hypothesis: If tensor completion models (CPD-S and NeAT), optionally aggregated via a random-forest ensemble, are used as surrogate models for lattice-design prediction, then they will achieve higher R² accuracy in predicting Young's modulus and mass-normalized Young's modulus than baseline ML methods (Gaussian Process, XGBoost, MLP), especially under limited or non-uniformly biased training samples, because tensor completion exploits the multi-way structure of the design parameter tensor.
